epigraph.text:
Suppose one of you wants to build a tower. Won't 
you first sit down and estimate the cost to see if you have enough 
money to complete it?

content.text:
Percents are essential tools for real-world problem-solving. In 
this section, we apply percent calculations to practical situations 
involving percent increase, percent decrease, simple interest, and 
commission.

When a quantity grows, we often describe the change as a percent 
of the original amount.

content.title: Finding percent increase
content.problem:
A town's population grew from 15{,}000 to 18{,}000. What was the 
percent increase?

content.title: Finding new amount after increase
content.problem:
A worker earning \$52{,}000 per year receives a 4\% raise. What 
is the new salary?

content.text: When a quantity shrinks, we calculate percent decrease similarly.

content.title: Finding percent decrease
content.problem:
A car's value dropped from \$20{,}000 to \$17{,}000. What was the 
percent decrease?

content.title: Finding new amount after decrease
content.problem: A \$600 tablet is on sale for 35\% off. What is the sale price?

content.text:
Interest is the cost of borrowing money (for loans) or the reward 
for saving money (for savings accounts). Simple interest is 
calculated on the original principal only.

content.title: Simple Interest Formula
content.content:
\[
 I = Prt
\]
where:

- $I$ = interest earned or owed

- $P$ = principal (original amount)

- $r$ = annual interest rate (as a decimal)

- $t$ = time in years

content.title: Calculating simple interest
content.problem:
You deposit \$2{,}000 in a savings account that earns 3\% simple 
interest annually. How much interest will you earn in 4 years?
content.solution:
$$
 I &= Prt \\
 I &= \__MATH_BLOCK_0__240
$$
You will earn \$240 in interest over 4 years.

content.title: Total amount with interest
content.problem:
You borrow \$5{,}000 at 8\% simple interest for 2 years. What is 
the total amount you must repay?

content.text:
Salespeople often earn a *commission*—a percentage of their 
total sales.

content.title: Calculating commission
content.problem:
A real estate agent earns 3\% commission on home sales. If she 
sells a house for \$350{,}000, what is her commission?

content.title: Finding sales from commission
content.problem:
A salesperson earns 6\% commission and earned \$4{,}200 last month. 
What were her total sales?

content.text:
Many workers earn both a base salary and commission. Their total 
income is the sum of both.

content.title: Salary plus commission
content.problem:
A car salesperson earns \$2{,}000 per month base salary plus 2\% 
commission on all sales. If she sells \$180{,}000 worth of cars in 
a month, what is her total income?

content.title: Biblical Wisdom on Finances
content.content:
Proverbs offers extensive financial wisdom: saving (Proverbs 21:20), 
avoiding debt (Proverbs 22:7), planning ahead (Proverbs 21:5), and 
diversifying (Ecclesiastes 11:2). Understanding percent calculations 
for interest, increase, and decrease equips us to apply this wisdom 
practically in modern financial decisions.

marginNotes.text:
God blessed them and said, "Be 
fruitful and increase in number and fill the water in the seas, 
and let the birds increase on the earth."

Dishonest money dwindles away, but 
whoever gathers money little by little makes it grow.

marginNotes.author: Carl Friedrich Gauss
marginNotes.text:
Mathematics is the queen of the 
sciences and number theory is the queen of mathematics.

marginNotes.author: Benjamin Franklin
marginNotes.text:
An investment in knowledge pays the 
best interest.
